title: Inclusive Interaction, Content, Responsive Design, and Human Evaluation · Evidence Recall
summary: A delayed review reconstructs inclusive interaction, content, responsive design, and human evaluation from stakeholder, decision, assumption, revision, artifact, observation, changed case, rejected failure, repair, owner, and residual-risk evidence. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for accessible responsive product experience after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Structure landmarks, headings, names, instructions, controls, errors, and status around the user task and reading order.
Verify complete keyboard operation, logical order, visible focus, focus movement, escape paths, and no traps across state changes.
Design content and interaction to reflow at zoom and changed mobile, tablet, desktop, orientation, text, and language conditions.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# personal-project-1 cumulative independent product evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic evidence-contract review only.
# They never contact participants or services, execute commands, deploy, or claim live conformance.
workspace: portfolio
project-id: personal-project-1
stakeholder: [replace with affected people and accountable decision owner]
decision: [replace with the decision this evidence changes]
problem-evidence: [replace with observed need, counterevidence, and uncertainty]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
security: [replace with threat, authorization, abuse, response, and residual risk]
privacy: [replace with purpose, minimization, retention, access, correction, and deletion]
test-strategy: [replace with behavior, changed case, fault, accessibility, security, and recovery]
release-evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, inventory, provenance, validation, and rollback]
recovery: [replace with backup, clean restore, reconciliation, communication, and ownership]
portfolio-proof: [replace with contribution, limitations, counterexample, and unseen defense]
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research, toolchain, deployment, and review environments
evidence:

steps.title: Separate evidence from confidence · Review Human Accessibility Eval
steps.instruction:
review case — The primary workflow works by mouse at one desktop width but loses reading order, focus, status, error recovery, and meaning under changed access needs. The review variation 2 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Combine automated checks, keyboard review, screen-reader or structured-text inspection, zoom, contrast, and affected-user evidence with documented limits.

Inspect the Human Accessibility Eval trace for this review case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: Separate evidence from confidence · Review Human Accessibility Eval.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for combine automated checks, keyboard review, screen-reader or structured-text inspection, zoom, contrast, and affected-user evidence with documented limits. For Separate evidence from confidence · Review Human Accessibility Eval, keep Human Accessibility Eval connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.text: For Human Accessibility Eval, distinguish a claim from evidence by locating the relevant precondition, trace or invariant, and changed input result.

hints: For Separate evidence from confidence · Review Human Accessibility Eval, name the types, ownership, and stop path, Human Accessibility Eval precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Separate evidence from confidence · Review Human Accessibility Eval, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: The primary workflow works by mouse at one desktop width but loses reading order, focus, status, error recovery, and meaning under changed access needs. The review variation 2 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Combine automated checks, keyboard review, screen-reader or structured-text inspection, zoom, contrast, and affected-user evidence with documented limits.

For Separate evidence from confidence · Review Human Accessibility Eval, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: One accessibility tool can certify WCAG conformance.

stimulus.title: Human Accessibility Eval case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: The primary workflow works by mouse at one desktop width but loses reading order, focus, status, error recovery, and meaning under changed access needs. The review variation 2 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Combine automated checks, keyboard review, screen-reader or structured-text inspection, zoom, contrast, and affected-user evidence with documented limits.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: One accessibility tool can certify WCAG conformance.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Combine automated checks, keyboard review, screen-reader or structured-text inspection, zoom, contrast, and affected-user evidence with documented limits. Artifact under review: accessible responsive product experience.

options.text: The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample. That claim does not survive the separate evidence from confidence evidence boundary.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.
The record names the assumption, applies Human Accessibility Eval, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

steps.title: Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review Semantic Task Flow
steps.instruction:
Order the Semantic Task Flow evidence cycle for this review task before implementing it.

Evidence target: Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review Semantic Task Flow.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Semantic Task Flow assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. steps.title: Record what required effort · Review Human Accessibility Eval
steps.instruction:
Write the review evidence note for Human Accessibility Eval: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — The primary workflow works by mouse at one desktop width but loses reading order, focus, status, error recovery, and meaning under changed access needs. The review variation 4 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Combine automated checks, keyboard review, screen-reader or structured-text inspection, zoom, contrast, and affected-user evidence with documented limits.

Evidence target: Record what required effort · Review Human Accessibility Eval.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Record what required effort · Review Human Accessibility Eval, keep Human Accessibility Eval connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Combine automated checks, keyboard review, screen-reader or structured-text inspection, zoom, contrast, and affected-user evidence with documented limits.

hints: For Record what required effort · Review Human Accessibility Eval, name the types, ownership, and stop path, Human Accessibility Eval precondition, or invariant that must hold.
